methods.description: Simulation of the global atmosphere using the Hamburg AGCM ECHAM4 with explicit cycling of water stable isotopes. The simulation is forced by observed sea surface temperature, sea ice, and greenhouse gas concentrations to generate climate data, including isotopic composition of precipitation, for a specific historical period. Ensemble simulations are also performed to study variability.

steps.description: The applied model was the Hamburg AGCM ECHAM4 (Roeckner et al., 1996) with both water stable isotopes H 18 2 O and HDO explicitly cycled through the water cycle of the model (Werner and Heimann, 2002).
steps.searchable_summary: Configure atmospheric general circulation model (AGCM) ECHAM4 with stable water isotope module enabled.
outputs: Configured ECHAM4 model instance with isotope cycling

steps.description: The simulation was performed in T30 resolution (3.75 by 3.75 spatial grid; 19 vertical levels).
steps.searchable_summary: Set spatial and vertical resolution for the atmospheric model simulation.
outputs: ECHAM4 model instance configured with specified resolution

steps.description: Observed monthly values of the global sea ice and sea surface temperature data set (GISST2.2) of the United Kingdom Meteorological Office were prescribed for the period 1908-1994 (Rayner et al., 1996).
steps.searchable_summary: Load and prescribe observed monthly global sea ice and sea surface temperature (SST) data as boundary conditions for the atmospheric model.

steps.description: Atmospheric concentrations of greenhouse gases (CO 2 , CH 4 , N 2 O) but no additional aerosol forcing were also prescribed according to the observations.
steps.searchable_summary: Load and prescribe observed atmospheric concentrations of greenhouse gases (CO2, CH4, N2O) as forcing for the atmospheric model.
inputs: Observed CO2, CH4, N2O concentration data

steps.description: The simulation was performed... for the period 1908-1994.
steps.searchable_summary: Run the atmospheric model simulation for the specified historical period.
outputs: Atmospheric model output data (climate variables, water isotope values) for 1908-1994

steps.description: With the same SST data and the same model, ensemble integration with three members were performed in order to study interannual to multi-decadal variability (Arpe et al., 2000;Grosfeld et al., 2007).
steps.searchable_summary: Perform ensemble simulations with three members using the same model configuration and SST forcing.

methods.description: Calculation or simulation of speleothem calcite δ18O values based on drip water δ18O and cave conditions, accounting for processes like mixing in the soil/karst and kinetic isotope fractionation during calcite precipitation. This method uses specific parameter values for mixing time, drip interval, and kinetic fractionation.

steps.description: The mixing of water parcels in the soil and karst matrix is assumed to be 48 months (Wackerbarth, 2012)...
steps.searchable_summary: Set the parameter value for water mixing time in the soil and karst matrix.

steps.description: ...the mean value of the infiltration related drip interval is 3600s...
steps.searchable_summary: Set the parameter value for the mean infiltration-related drip interval.

steps.description: ...and the mixing parameter is set to 1 (the latter two parameters are needed for calculating kinetic isotope fractionation according to the Mühlinghaus et al. (2009) (Riechelmann, 2010;Fohlmeister et al, 2012).
steps.searchable_summary: Set the mixing parameter value used in the calculation of kinetic isotope fractionation.

steps.description: Isotope fractionation between the δ 18 O value of the drip water and the precipitated speleothem calcite also depends on several conditions inside the cave, such as cave temperature, drip interval and supersaturation of the drip water with respect to calcite (e.g. Dreybrodt, 2008;Mühlinghaus et al., 2009;O'Neil et al., 1969;Scholz et al., 2009 (Riechelmann et al., 2011). ...the latter two parameters [drip interval, mixing parameter] are needed for calculating kinetic isotope fractionation according to the Mühlinghaus et al. (2009)...
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate kinetic isotope fractionation for speleothem calcite based on cave conditions and specified parameters, likely using the Mühlinghaus et al. (2009) method.

steps.description: Isotope fractionation between the δ 18 O value of the drip water and the precipitated speleothem calcite also depends on several conditions inside the cave...
steps.searchable_summary: Simulate speleothem calcite δ18O by combining drip water δ18O with equilibrium and kinetic isotope fractionation effects.
inputs: Drip water δ18O (potentially modified by soil/karst processes using mixing time from Step 1)
Equilibrium isotope fractionation calculation (depends on cave temperature)
outputs: Simulated speleothem calcite δ18O time series
